reasoning: Tesla's track record of meeting self-driving timelines is extremely poor (often referred to as 'Elon Time'). As of late October 2025, reports indicate their 30-vehicle robotaxi pilot in Austin still uses human safety moderators in the vehicles. While Tesla claims they will remove these 'by year's end', leaving this step until November or December affords an extremely narrow window to accumulate the 10,000 genuine, non-tester passenger rides required. To hit 10,000 rides in a month, a 30-vehicle fleet would need to flawlessly complete over 10 rides per car per day without any technical, regulatory, or public setbacks. Given ongoing NHTSA scrutiny and the stringent market criteria (no backup drivers, no observers, non-tester public rides, still operational at year end), the probability of hitting 10,000 completely unsupervised rides by Dec 31, 2025, is exceptionally low.
batch_queries: Will Tesla have driverless ride-hailing in the US by end of year?
